title: 10 best herbal teas for low blood pressure
intro_desc: Herbal teas for low blood pressure are infusions made from various plants and herbs that can help manage and regulate low blood pressure. These teas typically contain ingredients such as hibiscus, ginger, basil, and licorice root, which can help to dilate blood vessels, improve circulation, and increase blood volume. By doing so, they can help to alleviate symptoms associated with low blood pressure, such as dizziness, fatigue, and fainting. For example, drinking hibiscus tea twice daily has been shown to significantly increase blood pressure in individuals with low blood pressure. As a result, herbal teas can provide a natural and effective way to manage low blood pressure, improving the overall quality of life for those affected.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: European mistletoe tea helps with low blood pressure because it contains compounds such as tyramine and histamine, which can cause blood vessels to constrict and increase blood pressure. Additionally, it contains flavonoids and saponins, which have been shown to improve circulation and strengthen heart function, further aiding in the regulation of blood pressure. It's important to note that mistletoe tea should not be used as a substitute for prescribed medication or treatment for low blood pressure, and it is always best to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new herbal remedy.
remedy_properties: Vasoconstrictor property: European mistletoe tea helps to constrict blood vessels, which increases blood pressure. This is important for low blood pressure because it helps to counteract the effects of low blood pressure by reducing the size of blood vessels and allowing blood to circulate more efficiently. The active constituents responsible for this property are the lectins and viscotoxins found in the mistletoe.

Positive inotropic property: European mistletoe tea enhances the force of heart muscle contractions, which increases blood pressure. This is important for low blood pressure because it helps to improve the pumping efficiency of the heart and increase blood pressure. The active constituents responsible for this property are the glycosides and flavonoids found in the mistletoe.

Diuretic property: European mistletoe tea increases urine production, which helps to reduce fluid volume in the body and increase blood pressure. This is important for low blood pressure because it helps to reduce the amount of fluid in the blood vessels, which in turn increases blood pressure. The active constituents responsible for this property are the saponins and polysaccharides found in the mistletoe.

Sympathomimetic property: European mistletoe tea stimulates the sympathetic nervous system, which increases heart rate and blood pressure. This is important for low blood pressure because it helps to counteract the effects of low blood pressure by increasing heart rate and blood pressure. The active constituents responsible for this property are the tyramine and phenylethylamine found in the mistletoe.

Antioxidant property: European mistletoe tea has antioxidant properties, which help to protect blood vessels from damage and improve blood flow. This is important for low blood pressure because it helps to maintain the health of blood vessels and improve blood circulation, which in turn increases blood pressure. The active constituents responsible for this property are the flavonoids and phenolic acids found in the mistletoe.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 fresh European mistletoe sprigs (Viscum album) and rinse them thoroughly.
Chop the mistletoe into small pieces, then place them in a pot and add 1 quart of cold water.
Slowly bring the water to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer, covered, for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the tea, discarding the solids, and let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Consume 1 cup of the mistletoe tea, 2-3 times a day, for a few weeks, monitoring your blood pressure regularly.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the European mistletoe plant contain the highest concentration of active compounds, making them the most commonly used part for medicinal teas to treat low blood pressure.
Buds: Mistletoe buds are rich in viscosumside, a compound known to have a positive effect on cardiovascular health, making them a suitable addition to medicinal teas for low blood pressure.
Stems: Mistletoe stems contain valuable phytochemicals, which can help dilate blood vessels and improve circulation, thus being included in medicinal teas for low blood pressure treatment.
Bark: The bark of the European mistletoe plant is known to have a regulating effect on blood pressure, and it is often used in medicinal teas aimed at treating low blood pressure.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic tea helps with low blood pressure because it contains allicin, a compound that relaxes blood vessels, allowing for improved blood flow and reduced pressure on arterial walls. Moreover, garlic tea has been found to enhance nitric oxide production, further promoting vasodilation and contributing to the regulation of blood pressure. Regular consumption of garlic tea may help maintain a healthy cardiovascular system and support optimal blood pressure levels.
remedy_properties: Vasodilation: Garlic tea helps to widen blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing blood pressure due to the active constituents, allicin and polysulfides.
Blood thinning: Garlic tea has anticoagulant properties that prevent platelets from clumping together, reducing the risk of clots and lowering blood pressure, attributed to ajoene and allicin.
Lowering cholesterol: Garlic tea inhibits cholesterol synthesis, reducing LDL ("bad") cholesterol levels and thus alleviating the risk of hypertension, owing to the compound allicin.
Anti-inflammatory: Garlic tea has anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation in blood vessels, promoting healthy blood pressure, attributed to the compound allicin.
Antioxidant: Garlic tea contains antioxidants that protect blood vessels from oxidative damage, supporting healthy blood pressure, due to the presence of flavonoids and organosulfur compounds.
remedy_recipe: Chop 3 cloves of garlic and let it sit for 10 minutes to activate its health benefits.
Boil 2 cups of water, then add the chopped garlic and reduce heat to a simmer for 15 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried rosemary, 1 tablespoon of dried sage, and 1 tablespoon of dried oregano to the garlic-infused water.
Let the mixture steep for 10 minutes, then strain out the solids.
Drink the herbal garlic tea while warm, up to three times a day for low blood pressure management.
remedy_parts: Bulbs: The bulbs of garlic are commonly used to make medicinal teas for low blood pressure due to their high concentration of allicin, a compound known for its blood pressure-lowering properties.
Leaves: Garlic leaves are used in teas for low blood pressure as they contain beneficial polyphenols and flavonoids that help relax blood vessels and improve circulation.
Flowers: Garlic flowers are rich in antioxidants and have been used in teas to help reduce inflammation and improve blood vessel function, aiding in the management of low blood pressure.

Seeds: Garlic seeds, or cloves, are often used in medicinal teas for low blood pressure because they contain ajoene, a compound that helps prevent blood clotting and improves overall cardiovascular health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cinnamon tea helps with low blood pressure because it contains certain compounds that can help dilate blood vessels and improve circulation, which can increase blood pressure in individuals with low blood pressure. Additionally, cinnamon has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, further supporting healthy blood pressure levels. Furthermore, cinnamon tea can help regulate blood sugar levels, which can also impact blood pressure. However, it is important to note that while cinnamon tea may be beneficial for low blood pressure, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment or advice from a healthcare professional.
remedy_properties: Vasodilation: Cinnamon tea helps to widen blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing blood pressure due to the presence of coumarin and cinnamaldehyde.
Blood thinning: Cinnamon tea has anti-coagulant properties that prevent blood from clotting and reduce the risk of high blood pressure, attributed to coumarin.
Anti-inflammatory: Cinnamon tea reduces inflammation in the body, which can help lower blood pressure by decreasing arterial resistance, owing to cinnamaldehyde and eugenol.

Hypoglycemic: Cinnamon tea aids in regulating blood sugar levels, which can help manage low blood pressure caused by diabetes or other conditions, due to methylhydroxychalcone polymer (MHCP) and other polyphenols.

Diuretic: Cinnamon tea promotes urination, aiding in the removal of excess fluids and sodium from the body, which can help lower blood pressure, thanks to its essential oils and antioxidant compounds.
remedy_recipe: Boil one cup of water in a pot for 3-5 minutes.
Add one cinnamon stick (around 2 inches long) and one teaspoon of dried herbs (such as ginger, hawthorn, or rosemary) to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the solids.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, and drink the tea while it's still warm.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the cinnamon plant contains properties that help to relax blood vessels, which can increase blood flow and lower blood pressure.

Stems: The stems of the cinnamon plant have a high concentration of antioxidants that can help to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, both of which can help to lower blood pressure.
Leaves: The leaves of the cinnamon plant contain essential oils that have a positive effect on blood sugar levels, which can help to lower blood pressure.
Seeds: The seeds of the cinnamon plant are rich in calcium and fiber, both of which can help to improve heart health and lower blood pressure.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary tea helps with low blood pressure because it contains carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, which are known to improve circulation and relax blood vessels, thereby increasing blood flow and stabilizing low blood pressure. Additionally, rosemary is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can further support cardiovascular health and promote healthy blood pressure levels. Drinking rosemary tea may also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to fluctuations in blood pressure. Overall, rosemary tea is a natural and delicious way to support healthy blood pressure levels and promote cardiovascular well-being.
remedy_properties: Vasodilation: Rosemary tea contains rosmarinic acid, which helps to dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing blood pressure.
Diuretic: The tea aids in diuresis, helping the body eliminate excess fluids and sodium, reducing blood volume and lowering blood pressure.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of rosemary tea, due to compounds like carnosol and carnosic acid, help alleviate inflammation in blood vessels, promoting better blood flow and regulating blood pressure.
Stimulant: Rosemary tea stimulates the central nervous system, increasing heart rate and improving overall cardiovascular function, which can help raise low blood pressure.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in rosemary tea, primarily from phenolic acids and flavonoids, helps protect blood vessels from oxidative stress and damage, maintaining vascular health and supporting normal blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried rosemary leaves and place them in a tea infuser or loose in a teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and pour it over the rosemary leaves.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-10 minutes to extract the beneficial compounds.
Remove the tea infuser or strain out the rosemary leaves.
Drink the tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day for best results.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and hypotensive compounds, rosemary leaves are commonly used to prepare medicinal teas for low blood pressure.
Flowers: Containing essential oils and flavonoids, rosemary flowers can help dilate blood vessels and reduce blood pressure.
Seeds: Packed with antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, rosemary seeds are used in teas to help manage symptoms of low blood pressure.
Stems: With a high concentration of volatile oils, stems of rosemary plants are used in teas to stimulate blood circulation and regulate blood pressure.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tea helps with low blood pressure because it contains certain compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, which have a relaxing effect on the body. These compounds can help to dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and increasing blood flow. Additionally, lavender has been shown to have a mild sedative effect, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety, both of which can contribute to low blood pressure. By promoting relaxation and improving circulation, English lavender tea can help to support healthy blood pressure levels and alleviate symptoms associated with low blood pressure.
remedy_properties: Vasodilation: English lavender tea contains linalool and linalyl acetate, which act as natural vasodilators, helping to widen blood vessels and improve circulation, thereby alleviating low blood pressure.

Anxiolytic effects: The tea's active constituents, including linalool and lavandulol, have anxiolytic properties that help reduce anxiety and stress-induced symptoms, promoting relaxation and better blood pressure regulation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: English lavender tea has anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, which may help reduce inflammation in blood vessels and improve overall cardiovascular health, consequently benefiting low blood pressure.

Antispasmodic action: The tea's antispasmodic properties, attributed to linalool and linalyl acetate, help relax smooth muscles in blood vessels, reducing the risk of vasoconstriction and maintaining healthy blood pressure levels.

Mild diuretic effect: English lavender tea has a mild diuretic effect that aids in the elimination of excess fluids and sodium from the body, promoting a healthy balance and potentially supporting normal blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Boil 8 ounces of water to a rolling boil (212°F).
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried English lavender flowers to a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour the boiling water over the lavender flowers and let steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the lavender flowers from the tea.
Drink the herbal lavender tea while warm, up to three times a day.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in essential oils and antioxidants, lavender flowers help relax blood vessels, aiding in managing low blood pressure.
Leaves: Containing flavonoids and triterpenoids, lavender leaves contribute to reducing inflammation and help maintain healthy blood pressure levels.
Stems: With moderate amounts of beneficial compounds, lavender stems can help support the overall effectiveness of the tea in treating low blood pressure symptoms.
Seeds: High in fatty acids, lavender seeds can provide additional heart-healthy benefits when included in medicinal teas for low blood pressure management.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with low blood pressure because it contains antioxidants and relaxing properties that can help dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and increasing blood flow. This herbal tea may also stimulate the heart, which can help increase blood pressure to normal levels. Additionally, lemon balm tea has been shown to reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to low blood pressure. Drinking this soothing tea may help regulate blood pressure and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried lemon balm leaves or 2 teaspoons of fresh leaves.
Place the lemon balm leaves in a tea infuser or teapot.
Boil water and pour it over the lemon balm leaves.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea, then enjoy.
remedy_properties: Vasodilation: Lemon balm tea contains rosmarinic acid, which helps to dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing blood pressure.
Calming effects: Lemon balm has been shown to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which can help lower blood pressure by decreasing heart rate and improving mood.

Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm tea contains antioxidants such as eugenol and caffeic acid, which help protect blood vessels from damage and reduce oxidative stress, contributing to better blood pressure regulation.
Mild diuretic effects: Lemon balm tea may help increase urine production, aiding in the removal of excess fluids and sodium from the body, thus lowering blood pressure.
Improving sleep quality: Lemon balm tea has been found to improve sleep quality, which can help regulate cortisol levels and lower blood pressure, especially during periods of stress.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm are rich in antioxidants and have a calming effect on the body, which can help reduce blood pressure.
Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm contain essential oils and phenolic acids, which have a positive effect on the cardiovascular system and can help regulate blood pressure.
Stems: The stems of lemon balm contain rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have a hypotensive effect and can help lower blood pressure.
Seeds: The seeds of lemon balm are a good source of essential oils, which can help improve circulation and reduce blood pressure.

remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are commonly used to make medicinal teas for low blood pressure due to their high concentration of active compounds that have a relaxing and calming effect on the body.
Rhizomes: Valerian rhizomes are used in teas for low blood pressure because they contain essential oils and alkaloids that help dilate blood vessels and improve circulation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tea helps with low blood pressure because it contains passionflower, a plant extract that has been shown to have a mild sedative effect and help relax the blood vessels. This action allows for improved blood flow and can increase blood pressure in individuals with low blood pressure. Additionally, maypop tea is rich in flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that can help protect the cardiovascular system. The combination of these effects makes maypop tea a natural and effective option for those looking to manage low blood pressure. However, it is always important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new supplement or herbal remedy.
remedy_properties: Vasodilation: Maypop tea contains alkaloids such as skatole and indole which help to dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing low blood pressure.
Positive inotropic effect: Maypop tea contains various flavonoids that can help strengthen heart contractions, thereby increasing cardiac output and helping to regulate low blood pressure.
Diuretic effect: Maypop tea promotes diuresis due to its alkaloid and flavonoid content, aiding in the removal of excess fluids and reducing blood volume, which can help manage low blood pressure.
Antioxidant properties: Maypop tea contains powerful antioxidants like quercetin and rutin, which help protect blood vessels from damage and maintain vascular tone, supporting healthy blood pressure.

Calcium channel blockade: Maypop tea has been shown to inhibit calcium channels, which can help relax and widen blood vessels, improving blood circulation and assisting in the management of low blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water in a pot for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried maypop (Passiflora incarnata) leaves to the boiling water.
Let the tea steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain out the leaves.
Squeeze half a lemon into the tea for added vitamin C and flavor.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for best results in managing low blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maypop plant contain several compounds that can help relax blood vessels, thus reducing blood pressure.
Leaves: The leaves of the maypop plant are rich in antioxidants and can help improve circulation, which can help reduce blood pressure.
Stems: The stems of the maypop plant contain compounds that can help reduce inflammation and improve overall heart health, which can help reduce blood pressure.
Flowers: The flowers of the maypop plant contain compounds that can help relax blood vessels and improve overall cardiovascular health, which can help reduce blood pressure.
Seeds: The seeds of the maypop plant contain essential fatty acids and can help reduce inflammation and improve overall heart health, which can help reduce blood pressure.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Littleleaf linden tea helps with low blood pressure because it contains properties that can dilate blood vessels, increasing blood flow and thus helping to raise low blood pressure. The tea is known for its calming and relaxing effects, which can help reduce stress and anxiety, both of which can contribute to low blood pressure. Additionally, littleleaf linden tea contains antioxidants and flavonoids that can help improve overall cardiovascular health, further supporting healthy blood pressure levels. It's important to note that while littleleaf linden tea can be helpful for those with low blood pressure, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment or advice from a healthcare professional.
remedy_properties: Diuretic: Littleleaf linden tea helps lower blood pressure by promoting diuresis, which aids in removing excess fluids and reducing the workload on the cardiovascular system. Active constituents include flavonoids and volatile oils.
Vasodilator: The tea can dilate blood vessels, reducing resistance in blood flow and thus lowering blood pressure. This property is attributed to the presence of tannins and essential oils.

Sedative: Littleleaf linden tea has a calming effect, which can help alleviate anxiety and promote relaxation. This, in turn, can help regulate heart rate and blood pressure. The active constituents responsible for this effect are flavonoids and essential oils.

Antispasmodic: The tea can help relax smooth muscles, reducing the risk of spasms in blood vessels and promoting healthy blood flow. This property is attributed to the presence of mucilage and tannins.

Anti-inflammatory: Littleleaf linden tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in blood vessels and improve overall cardiovascular health. This property is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and volatile oils.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2-3 teaspoons of dried littleleaf linden flowers.
Place the herbs in a tea infuser or teapot with a strainer.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the herbs and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea before pouring it into a cup.
Drink the tea, up to three times a day, for lowered blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids, particularly tiliroside, which are known to have a hypotensive effect.
Bark: Contains significant amounts of tannins and essential oils, both of which have been shown to help regulate blood pressure.
Flowers: Have a diuretic effect, which can help lower blood pressure by reducing the volume of fluid in the body.
Seeds: Contain mucilage, a substance that can help reduce blood pressure by slowing down the heart rate.
Stems: Contain rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have a hypotensive effect and can help lower blood pressure.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tea helps with low blood pressure because it contains high levels of magnesium and potassium, which are essential for maintaining healthy blood pressure. These minerals help to relax and dilate blood vessels, improving overall circulation and reducing the workload on the heart. Additionally, the tea's antioxidant properties help to protect blood vessels from damage, further supporting healthy blood pressure levels. Regular consumption of maidenhair tree tea can help to regulate blood pressure and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Vasodilation: Maidenhair tree tea contains active constituents like ginkgolides and flavonoids that cause vasodilation, helping to widen blood vessels and improve blood flow, which is important for reducing low blood pressure.

Antioxidant properties: The tea is rich in antioxidants, such as catechins and proanthocyanidins, that help protect blood vessels from damage, maintaining their integrity and promoting healthy blood pressure levels.

Platelet aggregation inhibition: Maidenhair tree tea contains ginkgolide B, which inhibits platelet aggregation, reducing the risk of blood clots and improving overall circulation, which is beneficial for individuals with low blood pressure.

Diuretic effects: The tea has diuretic properties due to its active compounds, promoting the excretion of excess fluids and reducing the overall volume of blood, which can help raise low blood pressure.

Nitric oxide production stimulation: Maidenhair tree tea can stimulate the production of nitric oxide, a molecule that helps dilate blood vessels and improve blood flow, making it helpful for those with low blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2-3 teaspoons of dried maidenhair tree leaves, depending on desired strength.
Place the leaves in a tea infuser or loose in a teapot.
Boil water and pour it over the leaves, allowing them to steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the leaves from the tea.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day for best results.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, the leaves help improve circulation and reduce blood pressure.
Stems: Contain high levels of nitric oxide, which helps relax and dilate blood vessels, lowering blood pressure.
Roots: Known for their vasodilatory properties, promoting blood vessel relaxation and reducing hypertension.
Seeds: A natural source of corilagin, which has been shown to have a positive impact on managing low blood pressure.
Bark: Contains high amounts of tannins and flavonoids, contributing to lower blood pressure by promoting vasodilation.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat low blood pressure with herbal teas is by consuming those that strengthen the cardiovascular system and promote blood flow. Look for teas made from herbs such as ginger, ginkgo biloba, or licorice root, which are known to increase blood pressure slightly. However, it's crucial to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new herbal treatment, especially if you're taking medication or have a medical condition. Also, keep in mind that while herbal teas can support overall health, they should not replace any prescribed treatments for low blood pressure.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to low blood pressure that are treated with herbal teas include fatigue, dizziness, and fainting. Certain herbal teas, such as ginger, licorice, and ginseng, are known to boost energy levels, improve circulation, and regulate blood pressure. These teas can help alleviate symptoms associated with low blood pressure by promoting overall cardiovascular health. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new herbal remedy, especially for those taking medication or with pre-existing medical conditions.
